summary.intent: Implement GitHub issues #245 (ContextMenu) and #249 (MenuBar) as framework-agnostic core state machines in the ars-ui repository, delivered in a single combined PR targeting main.
summary.outcome: Both core modules were implemented with full test suites (unit, snapshot, spec-conformance, proptest), component and adapter specs were updated, and PR #679 was opened. The session ended with the coverage ratchet still failing CI despite multiple fix attempts; a final commit targeting the merged-LCOV branch gaps was prepared but not yet pushed/verified.

repo: Snapshot tests follow a per-part/per-branch convention — one small .snap file per named part or output-affecting state, not one aggregate fixture per component.
Agnostic core machines must use NoEffect and own no live DOM operations; adapters translate state into mounted-handle work, focus movement, and positioning.
cargo xtask spec validate and cargo xtask spec compile-snippets are required gates that must pass before committing spec-adjacent changes.

The post-implementation audit skill (`.agents/skills/post-implementation-audit/SKILL.md`) is a mandatory step requiring at least two 'anything else missing?' rounds and findings must land in the same PR.

The waiting-for-codex-review loop requires posting '@codex review' as a PR comment, polling reactions and GraphQL threads, replying inline, resolving each thread, and re-triggering until Codex leaves a thumbs-up.
cargo-mutants uses the default std-enabled mutation profile; `#[cfg(not(feature = "std"))]` fallback paths cannot be observed and require documented exclusions in `.cargo/mutants.toml`.

The project coverage gate runs on a merged workspace LCOV artifact that has a larger branch denominator than a crate-only local reproduction, so passing `cargo xtask coverage check --file <local.lcov>` does not guarantee the CI gate passes.
Issues #245 and #249 both depended on #237, which was already closed before implementation started.
spec/leptos-components and spec/dioxus-components adapter specs must be kept in sync with core component spec changes, including part names and event lists.

code.finding: Existing full Menu machine with item types, submenus, typeahead, snapshots, and proptest invariants; both ContextMenu and MenuBar reuse its Item, ItemType, DisabledBehavior, StaticCollection, and typeahead::State.

The highlight proptest utility module was loaded without the i18n feature gate even though all its helpers are i18n-gated, causing cargo-mutants baseline compile failures. Fixed by adding the cfg(feature) gate consistently.

Horizontal ArrowDown activation in trigger keydown had a match guard tying it to Direction::Ltr; RTL horizontal menubars would not open with ArrowDown. Fix: make ArrowDown activation unconditional and separate the guarded vertical case.
SyncProps on disable cleared focus but did not restore a roving tab stop when re-enabling; the first enabled trigger must be set as the focus entry point on re-enable when no focused_item remains.

on_target_keydown initially only handled Shift+F10 and used hardcoded (0.0, 0.0) coordinates; Codex review required adding KeyboardKey::ContextMenu support and adapter-provided anchor coordinates as a parameter.
SyncProps disabling an open context menu did not fire on_open_change(false) or clear the typeahead buffer. Both are required by the spec contract.
ContextOpen while already open did not reset submenu_open or typeahead transient state; reopening must clear those fields before re-highlighting.

workflow: Adding assertion-heavy unit tests to fix branch coverage can lower coverage percentage by adding more instrumented branch sites to the denominator than are covered, making coverage worse with each attempt.

The correct coverage fix workflow is: download the CI coverage artifact (gh run download --name coverage-diagnostics), inspect uncovered branches from that exact LCOV, add minimal targeted test assertions for those specific product branches, verify locally with the same artifact, then commit.
cargo-mutants can stall in a final 'cargo test --no-run' compile when other concurrent cargo processes are running in nearby worktrees; killing and rerunning produces a clean baseline.
Flaky CI unit tests (unrelated to the change) should be re-run with 'gh run rerun --failed' rather than investigated; this allows the coverage gate downstream to execute on the correct commit.

Local crate coverage (cargo llvm-cov test -p <crate> --lib) and CI merged workspace coverage (cargo llvm-cov nextest with full workspace) can diverge significantly on branch percentage; always verify against the full nextest run or the CI artifact before claiming the gate is fixed.
The 'ignored' proptest targets must be run explicitly with '-- --ignored <test_name>' and are separate from the normal test suite pass.
cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject must be run in the exact scope that matches the test binary; running it with a narrow filter can cause it to delete unrelated snapshots.

friction: CI merged-LCOV branch denominator is larger than crate-only local reproduction, causing three consecutive coverage commits to fail the ratchet despite local gates passing.
cargo-mutants stalled mid-run for ~45 minutes due to concurrent cargo activity in neighboring worktrees, requiring manual SIGKILL before proceeding.
Codex review required five separate round-trips (10 threads total), each needing TDD-first fixes, inline replies, GraphQL thread resolution, and a fresh @codex review trigger.
Snapshot split operation risked deleting unrelated snapshots when 'cargo insta test --unreferenced=delete' was run with a narrow test filter.
Thread resolution via GraphQL mutations required knowing the opaque PRRT_ thread IDs, which the gh CLI does not surface directly and required a custom GraphQL query.
The coverage fix workflow was not understood early enough; three speculative commits were pushed to CI before inspecting the actual CI LCOV artifact.
cargo llvm-cov concurrent runs collided on llvm-profdata when run in parallel, requiring sequential invocations.
open_items: The final coverage fix commit ('test(selection): cover merged coverage ratchet branches') was prepared locally and committed but the transcript ends before the push is confirmed and CI validates it.
Adapter components for ContextMenu and MenuBar (Leptos, Dioxus) are explicitly out of scope for this PR and remain unimplemented.
The post-review menu_bar mutation gate was not fully re-run after the Codex review fixes; only the focused unit and proptest suites were re-verified.

The 'not(feature = std)' timestamp fallback in ContextMenu is excluded from mutation coverage by a .cargo/mutants.toml entry; this exclusion should be revisited if a no-std target is ever tested in CI.
Issues #245 and #249 close automatically via PR keywords only after the PR is merged; they are still open until that happens.
